What I’m doing now

Last updated

July 3, 2023

As of July 3, 2023, I’m spending all my time on these things:

  • Staying at home pretty much 24/7 because of the COVID-19 pandemic
  • Raising 6 kids (15.5, 13, 11, 8, 5.5, and 1.5) and trying to stay sane (family blog)
  • Living in Atlanta and working as an assistant professor in the Department of Public Management and Policy at the Andrew Young School of Policy Studies at Georgia State University
  • Teaching data visualization, program evaluation, comparative public administration, nonprofit management, and microeconomics at the Andrew Young School of Policy Studies at Georgia State University
  • Working as a part time data science mentor for Posit Academy
  • Converting my dissertation into multiple articles and sending them out to journals + continuing my research on authoritarianism and international NGOs
  • Working on several articles on NGO restrictions with Suparna Chaudhry and Marc Dotson
  • Reading some sort of religiously themed text every day (books)
2019-2023 Md. Jubayer Hossain
